candidate_dossiers.experimental_intent: confirm the strongest exact-conversion or hybrid-conversion route first
expected_observations: Look for strong carbonate-phase growth alongside silica- or oxide-rich residual phases.
Framework signatures should diminish substantially if bulk conversion dominates.
Post-carbonation solids should be closer to carbonate-plus-residual mixtures than to the parent lattice.
pass_criteria: The treated material shows a coherent carbonate signal in at least two independent readout families.
The observed product family is closer to the exact-pathway hypothesis than to a parent-only no-reaction result.
The candidate reproduces the predicted carbonate-plus-residual family without needing a special pleading reinterpretation.
falsification_triggers: If treated material shows neither carbonate products nor the expected oxide-residual family, the exact conversion hypothesis is weakened.
If repeated runs fail to recover the same product-family direction, downgrade the candidate.
If the candidate behaves no better than the low-confidence contrast set, remove it from the reinforced lane.

candidate_dossiers.experimental_intent: confirm the strongest exact-conversion or hybrid-conversion route first
candidate_dossiers.exact_conversion_equation: Ca2SiO4 + 2 CO2 -> 2 CaCO3 + SiO2 (used here as a carbonate-conversion ceiling; the hybrid interpretation is that only part of this conversion happens before framework signatures begin to persist.)
expected_observations: Look for carbonate-phase growth while parent framework signatures remain partially visible.
Expect both product formation and residual framework persistence rather than full collapse.
A pass would show mixed carbonate and network-residual signals in the same treated sample.

The observed product family is closer to the exact-pathway hypothesis than to a parent-only no-reaction result.

candidate_dossiers.experimental_intent: test whether hybrid or restructuring behavior survives direct experimental challenge
candidate_dossiers.exact_conversion_equation: Ca8Si5O18 + 8 CO2 -> 8 CaCO3 + 5 SiO2 (used here as a carbonate-conversion ceiling; the hybrid interpretation is that only part of this conversion happens before framework signatures begin to persist.)
condition_family: moderate CO2 exposure with repeated intermediate scans
cycle challenge to separate stable hybrid behavior from drift
expected_observations: Look for carbonate-phase growth while parent framework signatures remain partially visible.
Expect both product formation and residual framework persistence rather than full collapse.
A pass would show mixed carbonate and network-residual signals in the same treated sample.
pass_criteria: The treated material shows a coherent carbonate signal in at least two independent readout families.
The observed product family is closer to the exact-pathway hypothesis than to a parent-only no-reaction result.
The candidate shows mixed carbonate growth plus retained framework signatures consistent with restructuring rather than clean bulk collapse.
falsification_triggers: If treated material shows neither carbonate products nor the expected oxide-residual family, the exact conversion hypothesis is weakened.
If repeated runs fail to recover the same product-family direction, downgrade the candidate.
If the candidate collapses cleanly into a different product family or shows no repeatable carbonate growth, demote it to lower-confidence.

candidate_dossiers.experimental_intent: test whether hybrid or restructuring behavior survives direct experimental challenge

cycle challenge to separate stable hybrid behavior from drift
expected_observations: Look for carbonate growth together with partial retention of the parent oxide framework.

A pass would show a coherent mixed product set rather than random degradation.

candidate_dossiers.experimental_intent: test whether uptake stays surface-confined rather than proceeding toward bulk conversion
candidate_dossiers.exact_conversion_equation: CaSiO3 + CO2 -> CaCO3 + SiO2 (used here as a stoichiometric ceiling; the favored interpretation is that real uptake may stop earlier, with carbonate growth concentrated near surfaces or defect-accessible sites.)
condition_family: cycle challenge for retention versus collapse
expected_observations: Look for carbonate growth at accessible surfaces while the bulk oxide framework remains largely intact.
The exact conversion equation should be treated as an upper ceiling rather than the default endpoint.
A pass would show measurable uptake without broad lattice collapse.
pass_criteria: The treated material shows a coherent carbonate signal in at least two independent readout families.
The observed product family is closer to the exact-pathway hypothesis than to a parent-only no-reaction result.
Carbonate evidence remains surface-skewed while most parent-framework signatures persist.
falsification_triggers: If treated material shows neither carbonate products nor the expected oxide-residual family, the exact conversion hypothesis is weakened.
If repeated runs fail to recover the same product-family direction, downgrade the candidate.
If the bulk lattice collapses into carbonate-plus-residual products, this is not a surface-limited control.

measurement_technique_matrix.purpose: Check whether carbonate and residual oxide families appear in the treated solid.
measurement_technique_matrix.expected_signal: carbonate growth plus the residual family expected by the exact-conversion hypothesis
measurement_technique_matrix.failure_signal: parent-only pattern or a product family incompatible with the proposed pathway

measurement_technique_matrix.purpose: Check whether carbonate signatures appear while framework signatures persist or collapse as predicted.
measurement_technique_matrix.expected_signal: carbonate signature intensity evolves in the direction predicted by the pathway family
measurement_technique_matrix.failure_signal: no coherent carbonate growth or framework behavior opposite to the pathway hypothesis

measurement_technique_matrix.purpose: Check whether treated material shows a coherent carbon-uptake or carbonate-release signal.
measurement_technique_matrix.expected_signal: repeatable uptake or release evidence consistent with carbonate formation
measurement_technique_matrix.failure_signal: flat response or irreproducible behavior across repeat runs

measurement_technique_matrix.purpose: Check whether carbonate growth looks bulk-like, hybrid, or surface-limited.
measurement_technique_matrix.expected_signal: spatial distribution matches the pathway family rather than random degradation
measurement_technique_matrix.failure_signal: no coherent spatial pattern or complete mismatch with pathway expectations

campaign_batches.objective: Confirm that the strongest exact candidates show repeatable carbonate-plus-residual behavior.
campaign_batches.promotion_rule: At least two independent readout families agree with the exact-pathway hypothesis for most candidates in the batch.
campaign_batches.failure_rule: If the batch behaves no better than the contrast set, the reinforced lane must be downgraded.

campaign_batches.objective: Test whether hybrid or restructuring candidates stay coherent under repeated challenge.
campaign_batches.promotion_rule: Candidates preserve a consistent hybrid or restructuring signature across repeat scans.
campaign_batches.failure_rule: If candidates collapse into contradictory pathways, keep them below the reinforced lane.

campaign_batches.objective: Check whether surface-limited candidates really stay surface-confined instead of drifting toward bulk conversion.
campaign_batches.promotion_rule: Surface-sensitive and bulk readouts agree on a surface-skewed carbonation story.
campaign_batches.failure_rule: If bulk conversion dominates, the surface-control interpretation fails.

campaign_batches.objective: Use lower-confidence candidates as contrast cases so the anchor lane has a genuine falsification baseline.
campaign_batches.promotion_rule: Contrast candidates stay weaker or less coherent than the reinforced lane.
campaign_batches.failure_rule: If contrast candidates outperform the anchors, revisit the calibration and packet ordering.

decision_gates.question: Does the starting material match the expected parent phase closely enough to trust the downstream readouts?
decision_gates.fail_meaning: Do not interpret later signals as pathway evidence.

decision_gates.question: Do at least two readout families report a coherent carbonate signal after treatment?
decision_gates.pass_meaning: Promote the candidate to product-family matching.
decision_gates.fail_meaning: Treat the candidate as no-confidence for this round.

decision_gates.question: Do the observed products and residual signatures match the predicted pathway family better than a generic damage story?
decision_gates.pass_meaning: Keep the candidate in the active exact-conversion lane.
decision_gates.fail_meaning: Downgrade to exploratory or contrast status.

decision_gates.question: Does the same pathway interpretation survive repeated exposure cycles?
decision_gates.fail_meaning: Candidate is unstable and should not anchor claim language.

decision_gates.question: Do reinforced anchors still outperform contrast materials under the same workflow?
decision_gates.fail_meaning: Revisit the calibration and packet assumptions before promoting any candidate.

consensus_hypotheses.statement: Ca3SiO5 should behave like the cleanest exact bulk-conversion anchor, with carbonate growth and residual oxide-family formation that are easier to interpret than the rest of the lane.
Ca2SiO4, CaMgSiO4, Ca3Mg(SiO4)2 should show carbonate growth plus partial framework persistence rather than immediate total collapse.
Ca8Si5O18, Ca3Si2O7, Ca2MgSi2O7 should remain chemically coherent, but they are more likely to show mixed restructuring than the anchor lane.
CaSiO3, Ca4MgAl2Si3O14 should help separate surface-confined carbonate behavior from true bulk exact conversion.
If lower-confidence candidates such as CaAl12Si4O27, CaAl2(Si3O8)2, CaAl2(SiO4)2 perform as well as or better than the anchors, the reinforced lane is overstated.

next_ten_logical_steps.action: Confirm starting-phase identity for the reinforced anchors: Ca3SiO5, Ca2SiO4, CaMgSiO4, Ca3Mg(SiO4)2.
next_ten_logical_steps.pass_meaning: The anchor lane is experimentally interpretable.
next_ten_logical_steps.fail_meaning: Do not trust downstream carbonate signatures yet.

next_ten_logical_steps.action: Run the first dry CO2 challenge on the reinforced anchors: Ca3SiO5, Ca2SiO4, CaMgSiO4, Ca3Mg(SiO4)2.
next_ten_logical_steps.pass_meaning: The exact-conversion lane produces initial carbonate evidence.
next_ten_logical_steps.fail_meaning: The reinforced lane may only be a ranking artifact.

next_ten_logical_steps.action: Repeat the anchor challenge under humidified conditions for Ca3SiO5, Ca2SiO4, CaMgSiO4, Ca3Mg(SiO4)2.
next_ten_logical_steps.pass_meaning: Carbonate growth survives a second challenge mode.

next_ten_logical_steps.action: Run post-treatment phase scans and compare against exact equations for Ca3SiO5, Ca2SiO4, CaMgSiO4, Ca3Mg(SiO4)2.
next_ten_logical_steps.pass_meaning: Product families support the exact hypotheses.
next_ten_logical_steps.fail_meaning: Downgrade any anchor that misses its own product family.

next_ten_logical_steps.action: Challenge the plausible restructuring tier: Ca8Si5O18, Ca3Si2O7, Ca2MgSi2O7, Ca5Si(PO6)2.
next_ten_logical_steps.pass_meaning: Hybrid or restructuring behavior remains coherent.
next_ten_logical_steps.fail_meaning: These candidates stay below the anchor lane.

next_ten_logical_steps.action: Run cycle-based repeatability checks on the plausible tier: Ca8Si5O18, Ca3Si2O7, Ca2MgSi2O7, Ca5Si(PO6)2.
next_ten_logical_steps.fail_meaning: The candidates are too unstable for promotion.

next_ten_logical_steps.action: Run the surface-control packet on CaSiO3, Ca4MgAl2Si3O14.
next_ten_logical_steps.pass_meaning: Surface-limited behavior is distinguishable from bulk conversion.
next_ten_logical_steps.fail_meaning: The packet cannot separate surface and bulk stories cleanly.

next_ten_logical_steps.action: Run the low-confidence contrast set: CaAl12Si4O27, CaAl2(Si3O8)2, CaAl2(SiO4)2, CaSi2(BO4)2.
next_ten_logical_steps.pass_meaning: The contrast set stays weaker than the anchors.

next_ten_logical_steps.action: Update the calibration artifact with observed outcomes and compare promotion/demotion decisions.
next_ten_logical_steps.pass_meaning: The internal ranking survives contact with the first experiment packet.
next_ten_logical_steps.fail_meaning: The calibration needs revision before further claim hardening.

next_ten_logical_steps.action: Re-run the carbon-lane regression check so future edits cannot silently break the reinforced exact packet.
next_ten_logical_steps.pass_meaning: The AI scientist now has a stronger self-check loop.
next_ten_logical_steps.fail_meaning: The repo can drift without noticing.

packet_boundary_note: This packet translates the reinforced exact lane into a concrete experimental and falsification workflow. It is still an internal planning artifact rather than real lab evidence.
